citrus_black_spot: Prune for airflow, clear fallen fruit, and apply copper or strobilurin fungicides from petal fall through summer.
citrus_canker: Prune infected wood, disinfect tools with 70% alcohol, and apply copper bactericides. Remove tree if infection is systemic.
citrus_foliage_damage: Apply horticultural oil or soap for pests. Improve nutrition/irrigation. Use copper fungicide if fungal spots appear.
citrus_greening: No cure. If not miskaten for "Nutrient Deficiency", control psyllid vectors with Imidacloprid (avoid bloom). Remove infected trees to prevent spread; consult local agricultural extension for confirmation.
citrus_healthy: No treatment required. Maintain regular watering and fertilization.
citrus_melanose: Prune dead wood to remove inoculum. Apply copper fungicide during early fruit development (avoid heat over 32°C).
mango_anthracnose: Remove infected debris. Spray copper fungicide or Mancozeb every 10-14 days during wet weather or flowering.
mango_bacterial_canker: Prune infected limbs and disinfect tools. Apply copper-based bactericides to protect new growth.
mango_cutting_weevil: Collect and burn infested shoots. Apply Spinosad or Imidacloprid only if infestation is severe and trees aren't blooming.
mango_die_back: Prune 10cm below diseased wood. Disinfect tools between cuts. Apply copper fungicide or Carbendazim to wounds.
mango_gall_midge: Prune and destroy infested leaves immediately. Use systemic insecticides like Imidacloprid during early flush.
mango_healthy: No treatment required. Monitor for pests during new leaf flushes.
mango_powdery_mildew: Remove infected panicles. Spray wettable sulfur or potassium bicarbonate every 7-10 days. Do not mix sulfur with oil.
mango_sooty_mould: Treat underlying pests (aphids/scale) with neem oil. Wash leaves with mild soap to restore photosynthesis.
